decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime. While the breach of fiduciary duty is serious, the defendant has demonstrated remorse and has made a partial restitution. In accordance with sentencing guidelines for non-violent white-collar, first-time offenses involving amounts under $50,000, a short period of incarceration is deemed appropriate to satisfy principles of general deterrence while acknowledging the lack of prior criminal history.
